title: Fermented Crisp Jamaican Saltfish Salad
description: A tangy, fermented cabbage and saltfish salad bursting with Jamaican flavors and a hint of black cardamom.
instructions: In a bowl, combine the saltfish with 1 tsp salt, ½ tsp pepper, a pinch of crushed black cardamom pods, and ½ cup warm water. Let it soak for 15 minutes to rehydrate.
In a separate large bowl, whisk together 2 tbsp olive oil, juice of 1 lime, 1 tsp chopped thyme, and a pinch of black cardamom.
Add the sliced red cabbage, diced boiled sweet potato, sliced red onion, and chopped coriander to the dressing. Toss until evenly coated.
Transfer the mixture to a clean jar or airtight container. Add the soaked saltfish on top, mashing it slightly to mix with the flavors.
Seal the container and leave it at room temperature for 1–2 hours to allow the cabbage to ferment slightly, developing a tangy flavor.
After fermentation, refrigerate the salad for at least 30 minutes before serving to chill and let the flavors meld.
Serve chilled as a crisp, refreshing lunch, garnished with a few extra coriander leaves if desired.
